title: GLYCERIDE COMPOSITION OF TOBACCO SEED OIL
author: C. VENKATARAO
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The glyceride structure of tobacco seed oil has been determined by brominating the neutral oil followed by isolation of the various individual bromoglycerides by extraction with suitable solvents and estimating the fatty acid composition  of  these fractions The component glycerides of the oil have been found to be trilinolin (7%), oleodilinolin (35%), dialeolinolin (7%), myristodilinolin (2%), palmitodilinolin (9%), stearodilinolin (6%), myristoleolinolin (A), palmitoleolinolin (18%), stearoleolinolin (11%). The fat conforms to the usual evenly distributed type in the structure of its component glycerides.
steps_raw: The glyceride structure of tobacco seed oil has been determined by brominating the neutral oil followed by isolation of the various individual bromoglycerides by extraction with suitable solvents and estimating the fatty acid composition  of  these fractions The component glycerides of the oil have been found to be trilinolin (7%), oleodilinolin (35%), dialeolinolin (7%), myristodilinolin (2%), palmitodilinolin (9%), stearodilinolin (6%), myristoleolinolin (A), palmitoleolinolin (18%), stearoleolinolin (11%). The fat conforms to the usual evenly distributed type in the structure of its component glycerides.
